query: US spot Bitcoin ETF daily net flows June 2026 Farside Investors recent data
answer:
## US Spot Bitcoin ETF Daily Net Flows — June 2026 (Farside Investors Data)

### Overview

US spot Bitcoin ETFs experienced a historically severe outflow episode in late May through early June 2026. The data below is sourced primarily from **Farside Investors** (farside.co.uk), the standard tracker for US spot Bitcoin ETF flows, along with corroborating reports from CoinDesk, BeInCrypto, BitcoinFoundation, and CoinShares. Data coverage extends through approximately **June 6, 2026**; live web access was unavailable to retrieve more recent days.

---

### Record 13-Day Consecutive Outflow Streak (May 15 – June 3, 2026)

US spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded their **longest-ever outflow streak: 13 consecutive trading days** (~May 15 – June 3, 2026), with cumulative outflows of approximately **$4.37–$4.4 billion**.

- **CoinDesk** (June 5, 2026): "13 straight days of outflows, shedding $4.37 billion"
- **BeInCrypto**: "13 consecutive days… $4.33 billion"
- **BitcoinFoundation**: "13-Day $4.4B Record"

### Daily Flow Data (Early June 2026)

| Date | Total Net Flow (USD) | Notes |
|------|---------------------|-------|
| June 1 | **−$483.8M** | Continued heavy outflows |
| June 3 | **−$396.6M** | Final day of the 13-day streak (Farside primary) |
| June 4 | **+$3.0M** | Streak broken — barely positive (CoinDesk confirms $3.05M) |
| June 5 | **−$325.7M** (est.) | Outflows resumed |

### May 2026 Monthly Summary

- **Total May 2026 net flows: −$2,406.0 million** (−$2.4 billion)
- Only **6 of 20 trading days** in May were positive
- **Largest single-day outflow: −$733.4M** (May 27, 2026)

### Cumulative All-Time Totals (as of ~June 1, 2026)

- **Cumulative net inflows since launch (Jan 11, 2024): ~$55.2 billion**
- **11 US spot Bitcoin ETFs** tracked: IBIT, FBTC, GBTC, ARKB, BITB, HODL, BRRR, EZBC, BTCO, BTCW, BTC

### Individual ETF Notes

- **BlackRock IBIT** remains the dominant fund by cumulative inflows
- **GBTC** outflows largely stabilized by 2025 after heavy post-conversion selling in 2024
- **Institutional 13F filings** (Q1 2026): BTC ETF institutional positions fell **17%**, from ~313,000 BTC to ~261,000 BTC — suggesting partly structural, not purely sentiment-driven, selling

### Bitcoin Price Context (Early June 2026)

| Date | BTC Price |
|------|-----------|
| June 1 | $73,593 |
| June 2 | $71,360 |
| June 3 | $66,650 |
| June 4 | $64,022 |
| June 5 | $63,796 |
| June 6 | ~$61,011 (365-day low) |

- **Fear & Greed Index**: 12 ("Extreme Fear") — 4 consecutive days
- **Distance from ATH** ($126,080, Oct 6, 2025): **−51.6%**
- **30-day price change**: **−24.55%**

### Analyst Commentary

- **Investing.com**: Characterized the $3.4B ETF outflow as "more cyclical than structural — a cyclical reversal within a secular uptrend"
- **Standard Chartered**: "Bitcoin bottom is near, market enters BTC buy zone"
- **CoinShares**: Reported **$1.67 billion** in crypto fund outflows in a single week (second-largest of 2026)
- **Coinfomania**: "Similar 2025 outflow episodes were followed by larger inflow surges"

### Data Gaps

- **Individual daily per-ETF breakdowns** (IBIT, FBTC, GBTC, ARKB, BITB, etc.) for June 2026 were not available at granular level in the retrieved data
- **June 7–17, 2026 data** is not covered — the archive ends around June 6
- Recommended live sources for latest data: [farside.co.uk/btc/](https://farside.co.uk/btc/), [SoSoValue](https://sosovalue.com/assets/etf/us-btc-spot), [CoinGlass](https://www.coinglass.com/bitcoin-etf)

---

key_facts: US spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded a record 13 consecutive trading days of net outflows from approximately May 15 to June 3, 2026, totaling ~$4.37-$4.4 billion in cumulative outflows.
The outflow streak broke on June 4, 2026 with a minimal net inflow of +$3.0 million, but outflows resumed on June 5 (~-$325.7M).
May 2026 total BTC ETF net flows were -$2,406 million (-$2.4 billion), with only 6 of 20 trading days positive.
The largest single-day outflow in May 2026 was -$733.4 million on May 27.
Cumulative all-time net inflows into US spot BTC ETFs stood at approximately $55.2 billion as of June 1, 2026.
11 US spot Bitcoin ETFs are tracked: IBIT, FBTC, GBTC, ARKB, BITB, HODL, BRRR, EZBC, BTCO, BTCW, BTC.
Institutional 13F filings for Q1 2026 showed a 17% reduction in BTC ETF holdings, from ~313,000 BTC to ~261,000 BTC.
Bitcoin price fell from $73,593 on June 1 to ~$61,011 on June 6 (a 365-day low), representing a -51.6% drawdown from the ATH of $126,080 (Oct 6, 2025).
Fear & Greed Index was at 12 (Extreme Fear) for 4 consecutive days as of June 6, 2026.
CoinShares reported $1.67 billion in crypto fund outflows in a single week — the second-largest weekly outflow of 2026.
BlackRock's IBIT remains the dominant fund by cumulative inflows among all US spot Bitcoin ETFs.
